name: Weekly AI Research Newsletter
description: Gathers AI research papers, model releases, industry news, and open-source tools from the previous Sun–Sat week, then synthesises them into a polished markdown newsletter. Triggered manually each Sunday.

inputs.value: Find AI and ML research papers published during the week of week_start to week_end (dates are in the upstream context).
Find AI model releases and announcements from the week of week_start to week_end (dates are in the upstream context).
Find AI industry news from the week of week_start to week_end (dates are in the upstream context).

Review all gathered material from upstream (research papers, model releases, industry news, open-source tools). Deduplicate, cross-link items that span categories, and rank by novelty, practical impact, and timeliness. Select the best items per category with a one-line editorial note each. Identify the single most significant story of the week as the lead story.
For each paper in the curated shortlist (from upstream context), research it in depth and write the Research Papers section of the newsletter.
For each item in the curated shortlist (from upstream context), research it in depth and write the Industry & Business section of the newsletter.
For each item in the curated shortlist (from upstream context), research it in depth and write the Models & Releases and Open-Source Tools sections of the newsletter.
Using the curated shortlist from upstream context, research the lead story in depth and write three components: Executive Summary, Lead Story, and Connections & Trends.
Assemble the upstream components (executive summary, lead story, trends, research papers, industry news, models and tools) into the final weekly AI research newsletter in markdown.
